steps.title: Identify the greatest common factor
steps.content: Looking at $5x^5 - 6x^4 + 8x^3$: the coefficients 5, 6, and 8 share no common factor other than 1. The powers of $x$ are $x^5$, $x^4$, and $x^3$—the minimum is $x^3$. So the GCF is $x^3$.

steps.title: Factor out the GCF
steps.content:
Divide each term by $x^3$:
  • $5x^5 \div x^3 = 5x^2$
  • $-6x^4 \div x^3 = -6x$
  • $8x^3 \div x^3 = 8$
Therefore: $$5x^5 - 6x^4 + 8x^3 = x^3(5x^2 - 6x + 8)$$This matches choice B — B is correct.
